shlex/shlex/eof.name: eof
shlex/shlex/eof.description: Token used to determine end of file. This will be set to the empty string (''), in non-POSIX mode, and to None in POSIX mode.

info.excerpt: Token used to determine end of file. This will be set to the empty string (''), in non-POSIX mode, and to None in POSIX mode.

shlex/shlex/escape.name: escape
shlex/shlex/escape.description: Characters that will be considered as escape. This will be only used in POSIX mode, and includes just '\' by default.

info.excerpt: Characters that will be considered as escape. This will be only used in POSIX mode, and includes just '\' by default.

shlex/shlex/escapedquotes.name: escapedquotes
shlex/shlex/escapedquotes.description: Characters in quotes that will interpret escape characters defined in escape. This is only used in POSIX mode, and includes just '"' by default.

info.excerpt: Characters in quotes that will interpret escape characters defined in escape. This is only used in POSIX mode, and includes just '"' by default.

shlex/shlex/error_leader.name: error_leader
shlex/shlex/error_leader.description: This method generates an error message leader in the format of a Unix C compiler error label; the format is '"%s", line %d: ', where the %s is replaced with the name of the current source file and the %d with the current input line number (the optional arguments can be used to override these). This convenience is provided to encourage shlex users to generate error messages in the standard, parseable format understood by Emacs and other Unix tools.
